items.text: As we have incorporated and may continue to incorporate the use of generative artificial intelligence and other advancing technologies, any breach, interruption, or security failure of those technologies, or any non-compliance with applicable laws, could have a negative impact on our business operations, results of operations, financial condition, or reputation.
items.rationale: Introduces a specific new risk tied to generative AI adoption impacting operations, results, and reputation, not previously disclosed.

items.text: Information security risks have escalated as a result of these emerging technologies and the heightened sophistication and activity levels of cyber-attack perpetrators.
items.rationale: Expands on existing cybersecurity risk framing by tying it to new technology trends and sophistication of attackers.

items.text: Cyber-attacks can include third parties gaining unauthorized access to our data, including sensitive information about our residents, as well as our vendors' data or information technology systems.
items.rationale: Elaborates on existing cyber-attack risk by specifying data types and third-party vendor systems as targets.

items.text: These attacks may occur through stolen credentials, computer malware, phishing attacks, ransomware, and other deliberate attempts to compromise information security.
items.rationale: Generic list of common attack vectors typically included in cybersecurity risk boilerplate.

items.text: We rely on, or may rely in the future on, certain third-party service and software providers to host systems, provide key software, and to supply and store our sensitive data in a secure manner.
items.rationale: Adds detail about reliance on third-party providers for data security, expanding existing vendor/technology risk discussion.
